titleStmt.title: SII 19.2: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 2. (A.R. No. 4 of 1907.) TIRUVIŚALŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records a gift of 96 sheep left in charge of one Brahmādirāyar of Peruveṅgūr, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruviśalūr-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a dēvadāna and brahmadēya on the northern bank of the river. The donor's name is lost. Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, which must have been named after a title of Parāntaka I (or Pallava Nandivarman III) included in itself besides Tiruviśalūr, the village Tirukkaḷittaṭṭai also (See No. 5 below). This might be an inscription of Āditya II Karikāla.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . koparakecaripaṉmaṟkku yāṇṭu 2 Ira
ṇṭāvatu vaṭakarai tevatāṉap piramate
yam Amaṉinārāyaṇaccatuppetimaṅka
lattu tiruvicalūrp perumāṉaṭiyaḷḷukku
tuy kaḷḷaṉ taṉ nontāviḷa
kku Erippatāka peruveṅkūr brahmātirāyar
vaiytta Āṭu toṇṇūṟṟāṟu Ivai
rippatāka kuṭutta viḷakku lohattā
y peruṅkuṟi perumakkaḷom paṉmāhe
śvararum rakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.2 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
